analysis.name: John Hopkins

unclear grading standards and instructions, lectures focus mostly in readings and are not very helpful.

Dont bother by asking him for help. Hell ignore your emails. Also hes annoying unprepared for lectures, especially for intro classes, which he should not be allowed to teach in the first place. one of the best art profs i've had. he is very knowledgeable and genuine. lectures are not mandatory but exams are based on lectures so go. TAs are amazing too. highly recommend!

analysis.name: Baki Guney Isikara
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Isikara was super understanding, and tried to make the best of online classes. He gives extra credit and does everything he can to make sure his students get As. His assignments mirror the midterm and final, so studying those and participating almost guarantees an A. He is very smart, but his lectures can be kind of slow and boring.

Professor Isikara is very understanding. He explains concepts very well, and I have learned a lot from him. He makes sure that class is not an added stressor. The quizzes we have are online, our assignments are group ones, and the exams are reflective of what we do in class.

My favorite course yet! I loved how Prof.Guney teaches orthodox economic theories prefacing and post-facing the assumptions, and even implications of economic assumptions. Many other economic classes often end up technical-heavy, but he facilitates discussions of social welfare and environmental justice––which I appreciate a lot! Learned a lot.

AMAZING PROFESSOR!!!! would take it again if I had the chance. Can tell he's very passionate about the subject matter and knows a lot about it too. Always added a layer of depth to the lessons and course!

Professor Isikara is very passionate about his subject. If you do not may attention during lectures you are screwed but he makes sure that his lectures are very interactive and engaging and if you genuinely have an interest in Economics and want to take it with a professor who cares take his class. analysis.name: Ibraheem Catovic

Professor Catovic is chill but his lectures don't seem to be too helpful. He tends to read off slides and his exams are 25 MCQ. HW and attendance count for 20%.

analysis.name: Jeannie IM

Great Professor, she was very kind. There will be a lot of group discussions, but most discussion topics are very interesting. If you tell her any concerns with your essay during your meetings, she will help you tremendously. Like every writing class, be prepared to read. She gives enough time for assignments. Prof. Langer is very easy-going and a fun person to get to know. The class itself isn't too difficult and feels fairly casual rather than strictly academic.

Very disorganized and not that open minded to personal opinions in class. It's also difficult to meet her outside of class hours, and she will send readings to do before class very last minute. Shes kind but the course overall didn't feel stable and consistent with the syllabus.

analysis.name: Karl Storchmann

review_highlights.comment: In 603 and 604, students will complete a realistic corporate advertising task in the form of a group project, which is a valuable and engaging practical opportunity. The professor is not only kind and approachable but also deeply cares about her students—she replies to emails instantly and will go out of her way to help students.

Students from different cultural backgrounds fit well in this class, and a wide range of opinions is encouraged. Class participation and discussion are a major part of your grade. In general, students and the professor will share the talking time. If you rarely speak or do not like group discussion, your final grade will be affected.

The 603 course includes an interview project, two enterprise-related projects, and several guest speaker lectures. Most assignments are applied: you analyze real situations, prepare presentations, and reflect on what you learned. Overall, the work focuses on using course concepts in concrete projects rather than only taking tests.

Not an easy A, there will be solid workload. I took Cross Border Insight 603 and 604, these are not “light” classes. There is regular reading, writing, and project work. If you are looking for an easy 4 credits with minimal effort, these courses are probably not for you.

Wang Laoshi is great. She cares a lot about her students; you can email her outside class. I took her Cultural Insights of Business Communication course. She gave us many opportunities like bringing in the Yamibuy CEO and other guest speakers. The homework isn't bad but participation does matter. Show up and be ready to share your opinion!

review_highlights.comment: Happily dumping endless hours of weekly homework, offering pathetic quantities of extra credit, expecting perfection on a test that the TA's don't understand, and refusing to curve the class grade, Professor Versoza is lightyears from encouraging students to pursue programming professionally. Joe needs to reconsider his curriculum and beyond.
He is clearly smart, but is not a good teacher. His method of teaching is reliant on giving you an enormous amount of teeth grinding work. Also he talks way too fast.
Lots of homework and difficult tests
Gives Lots of homework and focuses on too many details at exam. He has some sort of weird smartness that he would like to show off.

(AIT) It's not an easy A. He's not very friendly in office hours; best time to talk to him is right after class while he's still pretending to be nice. He gives so much hw without any breaks, even while we were working on the final proj he gave us hw. Assignments and exams were not easy. Time consuming class so do not take with other hard classes.

analysis.name: Joan Varnum
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 70 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Disclaimer: The class is hard not because of the material but because of who the professor is. She is a very nice; she is very hard-headed and only believes in one narrow interpretation of the readings she assigns. She will punish you for having a different opinion or perspective than hers. So naturally, her directions are unclear. HAVE FUN

You don't need to read the books to understand what she's saying and most of the people in class use Sparknotes anyway. Just pay attention to her lectures, however, she is an extremely hard grader and isn't specific on what she expects. If she doesn't believe with your interpretation, she declares that you're wrong and will receive a bad grade.
Don't take her!!!!!!!! There is so much work and she is not helpful at all!!!!

Joan is a good professor, really inspires u , but her lecture could be a bit boring when it comes to art history part.  Since tests include much detail about note and art history stuff, it's not easy to get A. So get ready for long text of art history. Take notes as much as u can during class. If you are not that into art history, don't take her.

3 essays with very clear feedback given. Occasionally can feel lecture-heavy. Attempts to make class fun with special ways of instilling the topics. Midterm and Final are only exams and are easy if the topic is studied. Great conversationalist and fun to talk to after class. Amazing prof who wants the best for students. Take her.

review_highlights.comment: She's the worst. Like, her whole thing is just making students miserable, and she literally never helps with anything. No matter what goes wrong, it's always somehow our fault. Honestly, everyone — students and even other teachers — just can't stand her.

Professor has proven to be a challenging instructor, creating an atmosphere that many students find intimidating. Her strict approach towards students often feels more like mental torture than a supportive learning environment. No care and respect for students' needs and concerns, making it difficult for anyone to seek help or guidance.

Prof Benvenuto is CRAZY. But she's also good at her job. Don't get in trouble, participate, and at least try, and she'll usually be good to you. But everyone in the hygiene department will throw students under the bus if need be to save themselves. She's no different.

She's more like a bully than a professor. She even allows and encourages bullying among students and protects those who lie. professors and advisor here treat students horribly without facing any consequences. We're paying so much tuition just to be mistreated by them.

Perio's class was awful since there was a lot of material to cover, and she did not know how to summarize the information from the book and teach what was needed. therefore, her slides are about 100-120 words per class, and she also says to read the chapter!

review_highlights.comment: I really don't think he's as bad as these reviews say; he has very interesting points of view that can make the class fun. PAY ATTENTION TO LECTURES! (although he does ramble a bit, making it hard). He basically gives away what he expects in the exams very clearly. The exams are very straightforward. He takes forever to grade, though.

Literally avoid at all costs. I never write these but I felt like i needed too. Class was bad and so unclear. Took literal months to grade and was extremely subjective as to what he felt like grading you on. Just bad. Do not take.

He clearly doesn't know how to teach properly. He got kicked out of the UN for pissing off Russians after a vote of no confidence in his country's congress yet he keeps bragging about his career as a diplomat. Not sure why he takes attendance when we're only graded on 4 multiple choice exams. Take IO at AD or Shanghai if u wanna learn something.

The entire course is graded based on four multiple-choice exams. He tries to trick you in the exams with confusing wording and sentence-long answer choices that are differentiated by only a word. Not worth the stress, don't take his class

Though the professor has a lot of field experience, he fails to lecture or grade in a manner that is acceptable. Grades are not released until the very last moment, and lectures are often long-winded stories.
